description: aiohttp is an asynchronous HTTP client/server framework built on top of asyncio. Supports both client and server.

documentation.example: import aiohttp, asyncio; async def main(): async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as s: print(await (await s.get('https://example.com')).text())
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cd toolboxes/aiohttp && python3 aiohttp.py --target $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST`. Further invocations from upstream: `cd toolboxes/aiohttp && python3 aiohttp.py --help`; `cd toolboxes/aiohttp && python3 aiohttp.py --help`. Async HTTP client/server framework.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the web category. Fits when the engagement needs: Async HTTP client/server framework.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: Async HTTP client/server framework.
